basics.name: Ivan Navarrete Jara
basics.label: PhD Researcher in Geosciences
basics.summary: PhD researcher specializing in lithospheric modeling and geodetic data interpretation. Experience in numerical modeling, InSAR data processing, and machine learning applied to Earth sciences and satellite imagery.

work.name: École Normale Supérieure - PSL
work.summary: Thesis: Current stress distribution in active rifts from space geodetic measurements. Supervised by Jean-Arthur Olive (CNRS), Eric Calais (ENS-PSL), and Derek Keir (University of Florence).
highlights: Development and calibration of numerical models for lithospheric dynamics.
Processing and analysis of InSAR data for tectonic and volcanic processes.
GNSS campaign data analysis for present-day deformation in the Natron Basin, East African Rift.

work.name: University of Florence (UNIFI)
work.summary: Research visit hosted by Dr. Derek Keir within the TALENTS doctoral network.
highlights: Investigated the role of aseismic processes in strain partitioning across the Natron Basin, East African Rift.
Combined geodetic and seismic moment rate analyses to constrain the seismic coupling of rift normal faults.

work.name: ACRI-ST
work.summary: Applied deep learning methods to satellite image analysis for environmental monitoring applications.
highlights: Developed an AI-powered pipeline for automatic land cover classification from multispectral satellite imagery.
Applied transfer learning and semantic segmentation techniques to remote sensing data.
Thesis: Current stress distribution in active rifts from space geodetic measurements.
Thesis: Analysis of fault reactivation in subduction zones using functional analysis and adaptive time stepping.

awards.title: OSPP Award — EGU General Assembly 2025
awards.summary: Outstanding Student and PhD candidate Presentation for 'Inter-event strain localization modulated by background stresses across the Natron Basin, East African Rift'.

awards.title: TALENTS Doctoral Network Fellow
awards.summary: Competitive selection into the TALENTS network — The Doctoral Rift Science Network for the Energy Transition, a Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions doctoral network.
